title: rubus sepiiformis
intro: Rubus sepiiformis, also known as the western thimbleberry, is a species of thimbleberry native to the western United States and British Columbia. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus sepiiformis include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Rubus sepiiformis can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
